title: Mark `mach_msg2_trap` and Watchdog
description: A deeper understanding of the relationship between mach_msg2_trap and the iOS Watchdog, clarifying common misunderstandings in crash stack traces.
content:
## Background

Regarding "mach_msg2_trap", related information is frequently seen in iOS crash stack traces. I looked it up for better understanding.`DTS`Go and find out more about "mach_msg2_trap".

```console
Thread 0 name:
Thread 0 Crashed:
0   libsystem_kernel.dylib          0x000000020fdb7030 mach_msg2_trap + 8
1   libsystem_kernel.dylib          0x000000020fdc8b18 mach_msg2_internal + 76 (mach_msg.c:201)
2   libsystem_kernel.dylib          0x000000020fdc8db8 mach_msg_overwrite + 484 (mach_msg.c:0)
3   libsystem_kernel.dylib          0x000000020fdb7524 mach_msg + 20 (mach_msg.c:323)
4   CoreFoundation                  0x00000001d5b50148 __CFRunLoopServiceMachPort + 156 (CFRunLoop.c:2622)
5   CoreFoundation                  0x00000001d5b512e0 __CFRunLoopRun + 1208 (CFRunLoop.c:3005)
6   CoreFoundation                  0x00000001d5b55d20 CFRunLoopRunSpecific + 584 (CFRunLoop.c:3418)
7   GraphicsServices                0x000000020c6dc998 GSEventRunModal + 160 (GSEvent.c:2196)
8   UIKitCore                       0x00000001d7de780c -[UIApplication _run] + 868 (UIApplication.m:3782)
9   UIKitCore                       0x00000001d7de7484 UIApplicationMain + 312 (UIApplication.m:5372)
10  Demo                            0x000000010309b748 ApplicationMain + 160 (AppDelegate.m:40)
11  dyld                            0x00000001f32fc344 start + 1860 (dyldMain.cpp:1165)

```

## Content

This article will delve into the relationship between "mach_msg2_trap" and Watchdog in iOS. Through explanation and discussion of these two concepts, we will gain a deeper understanding of their roles in application execution and system monitoring. This article will also provide some background information related to "mach_msg2_trap" and Watchdog to support our points.

### What's`mach_msg2_trap``mach_msg2_trap` is a common function in the iOS operating system. It's one of the mechanisms used to implement inter-process communication (IPC), allowing applications to pass messages and data between them.

Specifically, `mach_msg2_trap` is a variant based on the Mach messaging mechanism. Mach is a kernel component of Apple's operating system, providing a low-level mechanism for inter-process communication. `mach_msg2_trap`, as part of the Mach messaging mechanism, is used to pass messages between user space and kernel space.

When an application needs to communicate with other processes or the system kernel, it can use the `mach_msg2_trap` function to send and receive messages. The application can call this function to specify the message type, target process or kernel port, and the data to be transmitted.

It's worth noting that the `mach_msg2_trap` function typically doesn't appear directly in application code, but rather is used in system libraries or frameworks. Application developers typically use higher-level interfaces and frameworks that implicitly use "mach_msg2_trap" for low-level inter-process communication (IPC) operations.

In summary, "mach_msg2_trap" is a low-level mechanism in the iOS operating system used for IPC, based on Mach messaging technology. It allows applications to send and receive messages to interact with other processes or the system kernel.

### What's`WatchDog`?

3th-blog [^Watchdog-3th]
:   Apple's iOS watchdog system enforces performance requirements by monitoring launch times and app responsiveness. If an app takes too long to launch or is unresponsive for too long, the watchdog will automatically terminate it on the user's behalf.

Apple [^Addressing-watchdog-terminations]
:   Users expect apps to launch quickly, and are responsive to touches and gestures. The operating system employs a watchdog that monitors launch times and app responsiveness, and terminates unresponsive apps. Watchdog terminations use the code 0x8badf00d (pronounced “ate bad food”) in the Termination Reason of a crash report:

```console
Exception Type:  EXC_CRASH (SIGKILL)
Exception Codes: 0x0000000000000000, 0x0000000000000000
Exception Note:  EXC_CORPSE_NOTIFY
Termination Reason: Namespace SPRINGBOARD, Code 0x8badf00d
```

### Will trigger `WatchDog` when `mach_msg2_trap`?

Discuss with `DTS`:

>No, quite the oppposite actually. mach_msg2_trap is the mechanism the
system uses to deliver events into your app so, so you're not really
"stuck" in it at all. When the user touches (or otherwise interacts
with your app), your app will immediately return form "mach_msg2_trap",
since delivering those events is actually what it's "for".
>
>Similarly, the technical reason an app killed by the watchdog is that it
WASN'T blocked in "mach_msg2_trap" "often enough". In theory it's
possible for an app to block in "mach_msg2_trap" for minutes/hours/days.
 The only reason that doesn't/wouldn't happen is that events or other
activity (like timers).
>
> However, an app blocked in the standard "mach_msg2_trap" event
loop can't really be stuttering or Hung.

"mach_msg2_trap" is a system mechanism used to pass events to the application and does not actually cause the application to freeze. When the user touches or interacts with the application, the application immediately returns from "mach_msg2_trap" because passing these events is the core of this mechanism's operation.

* "mach_msg2_trap" does not cause the application to freeze.
* The technical reason the Watchdog terminates the application is that the application is not blocked in "mach_msg2_trap" frequently enough.

## Reverse Footnote

[^Watchdog-3th]: [ios-performance-monitoring-best-practices](https://www.bugsnag.com/blog/ios-performance-monitoring-best-practices/)

[^Addressing-watchdog-terminations]: [Addressing watchdog terminations](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/addressing-watchdog-terminations)
